app.name: Django Typer
app.one_liner: Use static typing to define the CLI for your Django management commands with Typer.

screens.name: Command Definition Interface
screens.purpose: Allow developers to declaratively define CLI parameters and subcommands using typed function signatures.
primary_actions: Define command parameters with type hints

rust_commands.name: define_typed_command
rust_commands.purpose: Create a Django management command using Typer’s type-driven interface definition.

rust_commands.name: enable_shell_completion
rust_commands.purpose: Install and configure portable shell tab-completion for bash, zsh, fish, and PowerShell.

rust_commands.name: render_rich_tracebacks
rust_commands.purpose: Optionally use rich to format exception stack traces in the terminal for better debugging.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add support for subcommands and command groups
week 1: Implement type-hint based parameter parsing
week 1: Integrate TyperCommand as drop-in replacement for BaseCommand
week 2: Enable shell tab-completion across major shells
week 2: Integrate rich for enhanced terminal output
week 2: Write comprehensive documentation and examples
acceptance_tests: A developer can define a Django management command using only type hints and have it parse CLI arguments correctly.
Rich-formatted tracebacks appear when exceptions occur (if rich is installed).
Shell tab-completion works for all supported shells after installation.
Subcommands are properly registered and accessible via hierarchical CLI syntax.
The TyperCommand class preserves all BaseCommand functionality and is a true drop-in replacement.
